rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are properly declared in the AADL model or its dependencies before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by ensuring all identifiers refer to valid AADL features or previously declared eq/helpers with correct types.
When AGREE reports 'named thing must be an expression with a type', remove or correct the invalid guarantee or helper declaration that references an unresolved identifier.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference identifiers that are visible in the current scope or explicitly declared within the same annex.
Ensure all expressions in assume/guarantee clauses have consistent and resolvable types to avoid '<error>' type mismatches.
Use only AADL-defined property definitions, constants, and data types in AGREE annexes to prevent resolution failures.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must resolve to valid AADL types (int, real, bool) and cannot reference undefined identifiers.
Guarantee and assume clauses should reference only visible features or declared helpers with matching types.
Property associations in AADL must be resolvable to valid property definitions or constants in the model.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new property definitions or constants in AGREE annexes that are not declared in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings instead.
Do not reference identifiers in AGREE that are not visible in the current component's scope or declared in the same annex.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
